transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hello.
Good.
How are you doing?
Sorry, my voice is cracking like a, like a teenage boy.
Are you fully recovered?
I feel better than I sound right now.
Last week was kind of insane.
I don't think I've had the actual flu.
Like I've just had bad colds that I called the flu when I was being dramatic, which I will never do again after being like run over by the truck that is the actual flu.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I caught like a cold.
I incidental, thought I were
End of last week.
And I was like down for the count this weekend.
And I was like, this, this can't be the flu.
It's not that bad.
I was like, oh, I feel bad for the actual flu people.
Yeah, it's wild.
Like I was had a fever for like three days.
Like I feel like I just like melted into the into my bed and like could like, you know, like a Bronte heroine who was like, I'm taking to bed.
Like I'll see you in a week.
Yeah, exactly.
So I'm, I'm glad to be back upright again.
Yes.
Yes.
Thank you for your patience as I catch up.
All right.
So I'll just jump right in.
There was kind of like two things I wanted to talk about today.
One was just the pipeline status.
And then two was kind of just recapping.
I know we sort of talked about 2026 goals, but.
But there was also a lot that was in flux.

So almost like this is what I was hearing based on that meeting, trying to define where we want to adjust the strategy, where we still need to follow up and see if you had kind of more information to help guide that conversation.
And then the last thing is that we do have Check That Access Now.
Today, I will give you a walkthrough of the platform, and then I can get your whole team log in so you can get in there.
Thank you.
The other tool really has been just melting my brain.
Sorry, what tool are you guys using now?
Brandlight.
Okay.
Yeah.
We tended to use Scrunch, but I've been in there a little bit.
Yeah.
It's just like so not intuitive.
Yeah.
It's really hard.
And like I said, like the meta of it all, of being an AI, trying to create an AI.
And I'm like, I get like lost in chasing my tail of like, what am I doing?
Yeah.
And it must be, I think.
I it's challenging.
I've had this with other companies too, where it just like doesn't get, it's really hard for it to get the context right in terms of what you actually care about.

But yeah, we had a very funny, our COO, our new COO posted, like someone slacked, a salesperson decided to take it upon himself to go in, look at our GEO presence, which I love, and said like, hey, we're not, we're showing up in all of the LLMs when I type like, you know, search APIs, we're just not showing up in Gemini, like, why is this?
And it's like, whatever, there's technical SEO things that I'm working on to fix it, whatever.
And our COO like chimed in and was like, let's rank first on all of them.
I was like, like, okay, sure.
I just did like a rocket emoji because like there's, I have nothing to like say to that.
It's like the equivalent of someone being like, let's make this go viral.
It's like, I have no control over that.
That feels like a very like C-level.
Like an executive level comment, like, okay, I'll do that for you.
I'm working on it.
I think Rocket Emoji is the proper response to that.
And then my coworker, like Slack me, was like, lol, that emoji.
It's like, I didn't know what to say.
That's what the crying blood emoji is.
It's kind of like the half, like half laughing, half crying.
That was me.
I was like, what does that mean?
All right.
So I think the first thing I wanted to talk about, I know we have a huge publishing backlog.
And, you know, taking a look in terms of reporting the early signals that we've seen have all been really good.
And I think that, you know, this is based on, you know, eight articles, only three in the last month.
And obviously some of it's just, you know, other things you're doing.
But it feels like finding a way to unblock that pipeline and start publishing a lot of content is really the fastest way to accelerate every metric that we're.
So here's question for you, Ben.
Based on the feedback that you're still giving, like, does it feel like more granular style feedback?
Do you feel like you're catching, like, and basically, is there anything in that that would make you concerned about having it be online without having that extra, like, more granular review?
Because I think one thing we found, we had a similar client where just getting content published was really challenging.

And we sort of were like, okay, well, what if we, you know, if we think about content as a living asset that we're looking to get signal and build, like, traction that compounds, like, what if we just published it all?
And we more, like, made a flag that once something hits 100 views, like, that some, whatever that number is, like, this is gaining contract now is when,
I tried with our designers, but unfortunately they don't want to own them.
So there's a lot of different like elements here that are contributing to just the sheer volume and the backlog of it all.
But that being said, we are hiring a content person.
So someone will be coming in to help me.
So this is a good task for them of like, go read, go edit, make sure they look good, ask who you need to ask, get them live.
So like that I'm excited about.
But that is also why I'm like, let's just start going through old stuff and like refreshing that instead because it's already been published once.
And so it's a lot easier to just get it out the door or take something that's already out and break it up into five blog posts and get those out.
Like that is why I think those would help push this farther and get stuff out the door faster.
There's also stuff coming up like apparently now we cannot mention any of our competitors by name in any market.
Marketing materials, which I learned yesterday and had to edit an entire white paper to remove any mentions.
So like just stuff come out that I'm like, now we can't do this perplexity comparison article at all because.
And what's the, like the thought process there?
Came directly from the CEO and he said, like, we can use their logos, but we agree.
I don't know if there's an agreement amongst execs at these different companies to not mention each other.
But he said, we can use their logos.
We cannot use their names.
So that's coming now where I'm like, so this perplexity article has got to get completely white labeled as like competitor P and change to something.
So it's like, there's a lot, we're constantly pivoting our like focus and our strategy.
So like me going back there and picking the articles that I'm like, okay, this one is relevant still.
Cause like, I hate to go back and be like, yeah, all of these are no longer relevant.
There are some in the queue that I'm like.
Like.
Like, like, these now feel not as relevant.
So it's just, like, balancing and trying to pick and make sure that they're impactful in the way that we want them to be.
Yeah.
Which sucks.
Okay.
Yeah.
Well, I mean, I get the open, if there's anything we can do to help, like, help you unblock that, you probably know better than I do exactly what that looks like.
Like, you know, with the goal to making sure you see the results, you know, the impact from the content that makes it worth you all investing in it.
You know, that is our ultimate goal to be able to kind of point to, like, you know, when we have halfway through our, you know, partnership and say, like, here are the results we've driven so far.
Here's the signal you're getting and here's why.
And obviously, we can't do that unless we're getting stuff live.
So I guess the other question is, would it make sense to explore other lanes of less editorial?
Real content, like a more programmatic play that would require less oversight from you.

That's also something we could scope out and see if there were other opportunities to transition from this, you know, editorial lane to a more programmatic lane of content and just like swap them out, essentially.
Yeah, the only thing I can really think of is like just the very basic like what is X, what is this content where it's like this exists, this is a fact, here you go.
Because like those don't really need SME review if it's like this is just factually here's what this is.
And then I can flip those pretty quickly.
So those ones, unless it's like specific to like what is a search API or speak specifically to what we do.
Like if we lift us out of it completely, it really doesn't need an SME review.
Yeah, I'm thinking like, I wonder if.
There's a play here, like, AI tasks and jobs, you know, like, if you target a pattern, like, AI for market research, AI for legal research, AI for content research, more definitional.
Yeah, the only thing with that kind of stuff, like, use case stuff, is that then it does get into the territory of, like, well, do we actually offer that?
Like, if we're talking about it, should we be able to say that we do that?
But, and that's where I'm, like, now I need an SME to come in and say, like, yes or no.
So more of, like, you know, what is a web crawler?
Like, that article didn't need an SME review and it's out.
What is P99 latency?
Did need an SME review because it's super technical and I just needed to make sure, but the SME review is very light.
She was, like, I left one comment and so now that's queued up and ready to go.
So more, like, definitional stuff or, like, you know, what is vibe coding?
What is, you know, developer terms that developers are talking about?
All that stuff.
I think that's...
That would be great and requires not an SME review, not a heavy lift.
As long as it doesn't mention us or doesn't make a claim of what we can or cannot do or imply that we can do it, it's really easy to turn around.
And does that feel, I guess that gets to my next questions about some of the 2026 goals.

I'm wondering like how that fits with, it sounded like your target personas might be shifting in a way that would definitely want us to take a look at kind of the strategy and what keywords we're targeting.
So I'm wondering, this might be a good time to touch on this.

Does it feel like you've sort of settled on that, like kind of top down, bottom up, and maybe what we do is build a lane of content that really focuses on that executive, where it's like going to be very definitional, very high level, very like AI laggard content.
Yeah, yeah, this is definitely what the director.
That we are going in.
Yeah.
So I would definitely suggest for this content, we focus only on the AI laggard.
Okay.
Very like definitional high level stuff.
It doesn't help the mid funnel stuff, obviously, because it is all very top of funnel.
But I have so much coming from other places that I think that's okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I think that's fine.
Competitive content as a foundation is going to be really hard when we can't mention any of our competitors.
So that's tough.
If there's a way like I've assigned in this one white paper, I just had to edit last night.
I had like competitor A, B, and C.
So if we give these like white label names to these competitors, it's maybe a different way.
But those ones absolutely need SME review and really, really intense, rigorous SME review.
So that's a little bit harder.
Like I have a...
Yeah, it also feels like from a keyword perspective...
Like that, a lot of that lane of content that's been super valuable is comparison articles, like top 10.
Yeah, exactly.
So I feel like it's not, I mean, we can think about it.
There's a different strategic approach, but those are the articles that get cited the most.
Like I wonder if one of the, like the goal, like having the check that be a tool that you have access to, like you can go in and you'll be able to see, like, these are the articles that get cited.
And what they are is they're all like comparison articles.
They're top 10 articles and they are all mentioning people, not people, like companies by name.
Like I wonder if that will, could arm your team with like more data.
Certainly, yes.
Like that'll be something that I say to the COO who wants to be number one in LLMs.
Is that cool?
We have to say people's names with a ruffle?
Yeah, but that's also, I was going to say is like, once we stand up.
The.
The most helpful way to support you would be to just kind of scope out what this top-down lane of content looks like and just build out a content calendar focused on this.

For now, make sure we build out this persona in the pipeline, do kind of like narrow our keyword focus to make recommendations based on this persona specifically, and then kind of build a runway of content where as you're, it feels like mid-funnel and, you know, competitive content is something you're still figuring out internally.
So starting here and then making sure we kind of regroup on this as it changes.
Would be the fastest way to unblock that content.
Does that sound right?
Yeah, I would also, like, I honestly don't think mid-funnel or competitive, I mean, competitive, having the data to support why we need that is a good start.
But, like, we can probably handle that internally.
Like I wouldn't worry about that stuff.
Like I think the focus should be top of funnel glossary stuff and redoing old stuff or breaking old stuff apart because those are going to be really easy.

And in terms of like a refresh, a prioritization, is that something that you would like us to do an audit and kind of recommend where to refresh or is that something that you would rather just pull together a list for us to action on?
I think a list, pulled a couple together.
I don't know if you saw that.
We actually don't have that much content.
So there's not that much, but a lot of the white papers, I think I just haven't added those yet, but those are good.
I was like, this can be five blog posts, this one.
So I think that's actually probably going to be like a deeper well to pull stuff from, but I can just add those in because there's really not many.

Yeah, think we, the first one we are delivering, we're testing it this week or not, we've already tested it, but I think that's the content we'll be delivering this week is the rag keyword that you had mentioned.
And then we're all.
So doing a white paper for you as well.
That's perfect.

Yeah, I think, is it, I know that for you improving, you know, the benefit of using GrowthX, which no one is questioning here, by the way, but just having data that's, you know, we're publishing this, I understand why that's helpful, but would it be possible?
Or do you think beneficial to, one, if I go back through the queue of stuff that's already written and no longer feels relevant, is that fine?
And then, two, like, maybe do, like, a Q1, we're good on content besides those two, I think, that you're working on because those are great, but, like, no more new.
And then, like, April, we hit the ground running with sort of, like, some of this new direction.
Is that feasible?
I just, like, it feels like this pile of content?
Yeah, no, I totally, I think, I mean, the way our current...
Engagement is structured, has us, you know, producing content for you on a weekly basis.
So I think the question there would be more to make sure we are meeting our obligations to provide value to you.

If there are other ways that we could do that, like I mentioned, if there are other types of content we could recommend, like we've built out, like very programmatic pages, like glossaries or, you know, support pages for other clients that truly, like, don't need.
And this is something we could look at ourselves and say, you know, if this isn't the right line of content, where else can we kind of provide that value in the meantime, would be my question.
Yeah, so we just had some team structure changes, actually, and I now own the web.
Oh.
So that's.
is kind of a library of prompts that are based on your category, and then we have custom prompts.
So right now, I think the signal is being diluted because there's a bunch of category-wide prompts, not all of which are directly relevant.
So once I pull those out, we'll see a better signal.
So then you'll see, you know, your position, sentiment, your citation, share.
And then once you go into your prompts, this is where you'll be able to kind of edit the prompts themselves and also kind of dig in and see.
So this little symbol is kind of just your category-wide prompts.
And then your custom prompts won't have that little check that symbol.
So let me get to your custom prompts.
Okay, these aren't loading right now.
So I will fix your prompts.
But once you go in, in terms of just how to use it, this will track, you know, your competitors here.
For the remainder of this quarter, at least, that would be helpful.
And one of those definitely would be like a report on just like what we're seeing as like a, maybe like a Q1 summary.
Like here's where we are and then like, here's where we're going and here's the content topics now going into Q2 that will address these concerns.
Okay.
So that sounds like in terms of just like outputs, it would be most useful for us to provide you.

It would be kind of re-looking at the strategy specifically targeted to that top of funnel persona and how we'd approach that and what keywords we'd use with ideally ones that require little to no SME review to keep the pipeline moving.
Just a report on AI visibility in general, what we're seeing now.
And I'm assuming, you know, recommendations for how we'd action on improving there specifically for content.
My guess is our recommendations are going to run up against like a few of the things you've mentioned.
manufacturing functions.
please
So we should still recommend them so that you're armed with what you need going into that.
And so that I think is our first.

And then thinking about if there are other ways we could kind of support that aren't just purely creating content in this editorial lane, understanding how backed up you are and sort of what's shifting internally.
Okay.
I think that makes sense.

I guess the question here for you would be, I know there's been like a lot of internal shifting and a lot of shifting in terms of, you know, what's valuable and, you know, the reasons you were, the people who brought us on might not even, you know, it might be different now.
Obviously, you're working with a different team.
So I'm wondering for you, like, looking at what GrowthX does, you know, what do you feel like is the most valuable way we can kind of plug in to support, you know, your team?
Going forward for the next quarter.
Is there anything we haven't talked about today?
No, but I think the LLM like visibility stuff is huge.
Like that is the number one question I get from execs and Justin of like, how are we performing?
What are we doing?
What does this mean?
And then like, what are we doing to get better at it?
So if I could say to them, hey, we have an agency that's like literally just focused on this right now.
They're monitoring our LLM and they're creating content to answer all the gaps and fill them.
Like that would be huge.
Knowing that some of the recommendations that will come will bump up against what they've already said of like, no competitor names or, you know, they might require SME review, which is fine.
But then I can say to them, listen, you want to be number one, you got to be able to say perplexity.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
I think that would be incredible.
Like having that also as like a different lane of content production is like pure LLM.
Like that's the goal.
Okay.
That would be great.
Okay.
I think that makes a ton of sense.
So for me.

For first things, I'll make sure like your check that account is cleaned up and the proper prompts are loaded and then record a loom just walking through your team in more detail what each thing is, what it means, and then get you access.
And then I will make strategic recommendations twofold, that top of the funnel content, and then also LL and visibility specific kind of how we'd action on both of those in a strategy refresh.
Yep, that sounds great.
And I'll go through the publication queue again.
And just like, if there is stuff that I'm like, this won't fly anymore.
Like, is it cool if I move it back to like backlog?
Or do you want me to comment on it?
Is there?
Well, I don't know.
I'll make a new status.

Like, if it's already content we've created, I'll just make a new status of like a hold, like a holding pen, essentially, just so it, so we know kind of which content we're actually focused on for now.
Yeah.
And then you can move it to that.
So we know what the true publishing pipeline looks like.
Cool.
Yeah.
And I would say like, I know there's a couple in the works, but besides.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align content strategy with internal shifts and unblock the publishing pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=103.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Content Focus:** Shift from mid-funnel to top-of-funnel (ToFu) content for "AI laggards," prioritizing definitional articles that require minimal SME review to accelerate publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot:** GrowthX will now focus on two strategic pillars: the new ToFu content lane and a dedicated initiative to improve You.com's LLM visibility, addressing a key executive priority.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1519.0)
  - [**Competitor Naming Ban:** A new CEO directive prohibits naming competitors in marketing materials. GrowthX will use Check That data to prove that comparison articles (which name competitors) are the most cited, providing data for Brooke to challenge the policy.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)
  - [**Immediate Action:** GrowthX will deliver a Q1 performance summary and a revised Q2 strategy focused on the new ToFu and LLM visibility goals.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline Blockers

  - [**Primary Blocker:** A large content backlog is stalling publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=264.0)
  - [**Contributing Factors:**](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**High Review Overhead:** Content requires extensive SME and design reviews, which are slow.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**Strategic Pivots:** Frequent internal strategy shifts (e.g., new competitor naming ban) invalidate written content, forcing rework.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)
      - [**Irrelevant Backlog:** Much of the existing backlog is no longer relevant, making it inefficient to publish.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [**Solution:** A new content hire will help manage the backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)

### New Content Strategy & Focus

  - [**Strategic Pivot:** The new focus is on top-of-funnel (ToFu) content for "AI laggards."](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This content type (e.g., "What is a web crawler?") requires minimal SME review, enabling faster publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=684.0)
  - [**Immediate Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** Scope and build a content calendar for the new ToFu lane.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)
      - [**You.com:** Provide a list of existing white papers to be repurposed into multiple blog posts.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1092.0)

### LLM Visibility & Check That

  - [**Executive Priority:** Improving LLM visibility is a top executive concern.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1622.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Role:** GrowthX will use the Check That platform to provide data-driven insights and recommendations to address this priority.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1624.0)
  - [**Platform Demo:** Bailey demoed Check That, which tracks You.com's position, sentiment, and citations in LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)
  - [**Key Insight:** The platform will provide data proving that comparison articles (which name competitors) are the most cited, giving Brooke leverage to challenge the naming ban.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=897.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1678.0)
      - [Clean up the Check That account and record a Loom walkthrough.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1678.0)
      - [Grant the You.com team access to Check That.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1678.0)
      - [Deliver a Q1 performance summary and a revised Q2 strategy focused on ToFu and LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
      - [Create a "Hold" status in the content tracker for Brooke to use.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1707.0)
  - [**You.com:**](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1707.0)
      - [Review the content backlog and move irrelevant articles to the "Hold" status.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1707.0)
      - [Compile a list of white papers for repurposing.](https://fathom.video/share/1Pv5zfZMEu_xFNqbb8uv3NXszPizjvDC?tab=summary&timestamp=1092.0)

action_items.description: Clean Check That: fix prompts, remove category-wide; record Loom for Brooke's team
Deliver Q1 AI visibility report + Q2 topic recommendations to Brooke
Deliver top-of-funnel strategy refresh (AI laggard) + calendar to Brooke
Create 'Hold' status; Brooke moves non-compliant articles; GrowthX pauses new content through Q1
